posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
LOST FRIDAY AFTERNOON GATHERING – JAMET, GRANGE DES PERES, RAVENEAU, CHAVE, PIGNAN AND ETC. - Eddie V’s - Tysons Corner, VA (8/17/2018)

Six of us got together for another rendition of the lost Friday lunch. Since it was DC restaurant week, we move the venue from Blacksalt to Eddie V.

Champagne

  • 2010 Larmandier-Bernier Champagne Terre de Vertus Premier Cru - France, Champagne
    Similar showing as the last bottle from September 2017. Delicious and refreshing nose displaying granny smith, pear, almond paste, a hint of brioche, honeysuckle and crushed rock. Excellent balance, generous refreshing white fruit, good acidity and mineral, and a long clean finish with a hint of almond and brioche at the end. Really delicious and refreshing. A hint of almond/nut adds nice complexity. (94 pts.)

White Flight 1

  • 2010 F.X. Pichler Riesling Smaragd Dürnsteiner Kellerberg - Austria, Niederösterreich, Wachau
    Intense energetic nose displaying pineapple, white peach, cooked pear, subtle lemon oil, spicy spices, a hint of petrol and crushed rock. Exceptional concentration, concentrated oily yellow fruit, a hint of sweetness, mouth puckering acidity, incredibly mineral and a long dense concentrated yellow fruit driven finish. This is a big scale concentrated Riesling that is drinking incredibly. Nothing shy about it but not overdone. (96 pts.)
  • 1972 Domaine Ponsot Morey St. Denis 1er Cru Clos des Monts Luisants Vieilles Vignes - France, Burgundy, Côte de Nuits, Morey St. Denis 1er Cru
    Served blind by me. The oldest guess is from the 90s. Medium gold, very youthful color for age. Expressive steely nose displaying subtle yet very ripe dry yellow fruit, peach juice, dry apricot, steel, saline, oyster jus, smoked meat and wet stone. Amy and Mark mentions strong ham and I agree. Amy guesses Jura Chardonnay which is an excellent guess. Excellent concentration, concentrated tertiary yellow fruit, mouth puckering acidity and steely mineral, and a long concentrated yellow fruit driven finish with smoke meat and steel at the end. The overall expression shows a good resemblance to the younger vintages but more complex which can be attributed to the proper cellaring. Really unique showing. (94 pts.)
  • 2005 François Raveneau Chablis Grand Cru Les Clos - France, Burgundy, Chablis, Chablis Grand Cru
    Not as expressive as the bottle from 4/29. Pop and pour, not terribly expressive, wet sox and, wet stone with only a hint of citrus fruit. With air, lemon curd, fresh apple juice, honey, rain water, saline, honeysuckle, honey and wet stone. Excellent concentration, densely packed lemon and apple fruit, medium acidity, strong mineral presence and a medium to long white fruit and mineral driven finish. It improves with air, the last dropping being the best. I highly recommend decanting. (95 pts.)

White Flight 2

  • 2012 Domaine / Maison Vincent Girardin Corton-Charlemagne - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru
    Expressive slightly modern nose displaying ripe succulent yellow fruit, pineapple, pina colada, crème brulee, seashell and subtle limestone. Nicely integrated palate, nicely layered yellow fruit, silky, round and fluid, good acidity and mineral, and a medium to long sweet yellow fruit driven finish with a hint of oak and mineral at the end. This is a solid, slightly modern, Corton Charlemagne that is drinking beautiful. (94 pts.)
  • 2013 Louis Latour Corton-Charlemagne - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru
    Is it the vintage or Louis Latour? Lean, slightly medium concentration and slightly muted. The overall expression is fresh and balanced but seems to lack energy. Correctly made and enjoyable but a bit pedestrian. (90 pts.)
  • 2010 Bonneau du Martray Corton-Charlemagne - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru
    There is a hint of damp dirty cellar that mostly dissipates. Medium expressive nose displaying lemon curd, a hint of peach, a hint of oak, sweet spices and mineral. Medium concentration, good acidity and mineral and a medium to long citrus and ripe yellow fruit driven finish. It feels a bit flat and slightly more advanced than expected. Need to revisit. (91 pts.)

Red Flight 1

  • 2008 Domaine Georges Mugneret-Gibourg Nuits St. Georges 1er Cru Les Chaignots - France, Burgundy, Côte de Nuits, Nuits St. Georges 1er Cru
    Expressive nose displaying decadent yet fresh red fruit, cherry pie, strawberry and rhubarb pie, crushed rose petal, a hint of licorice, caramel and limestone. There is a hint of sous bois developing already. Excellent concentration, nicely layered ripe red fruit, bright acidity, earthy mineral and a long sweet red fruit and flower driven finish. Showing surprisingly concentrated fruit for the vintage yet showing a lot of pure red fruit that you expect from the producer. Really delicious but can improve for another decade. (94 pts.)
  • 2005 Remoissenet Père et Fils Chambertin - France, Burgundy, Côte de Nuits, Chambertin Grand Cru
    Medium expressive masculine nose displaying ripe black fruit, roasted blackberry, black cherry, a hint of stem, tree bark and earthy mineral. Excellent concentration, packed black fruit, good acidity, strong slightly coarse tannins and a long ripe black fruit driven finish. Really powerful and structured, lacking finesse and a bit straightforward. The tannins reminds me the old school Faiveley but I prefer the pretty red fruit driven nose of Faiveley. Not really sure how this will develop. (91 pts.)

Red Flight 2

  • 2006 Henri Jouan Clos St. Denis - France, Burgundy, Côte de Nuits, Clos St. Denis Grand Cru
    Pretty floral nose displaying sweet red fruit, flowers, cinnamon, tree bark and earth. Excellent concentration, nicely layered sweet red fruit, bright acidity, finely layered tannins and a long sweet red fruit and earthy driven finish with a hint of cinnamon at the end. There is a hint of ’06 bitterness that is not bothersome. Drinking nicely but can improve for another decade gaining sous bois. (94 pts.)
  • 1992 Domaine de la Grange des Pères Vin de Pays de l’Hérault - France, Languedoc Roussillon, Languedoc, Vin de Pays de l’Hérault
    I mixed up the bottles and served blind thinking it was the 78 Burgundy. Amy and Mark comment that the color is incredible dark and opaque. Expressive nose displaying cherry cough syrup, cherry jam, cinnamon, pepper, a hint of sous bois and earth. Excellent concentration, packed dense jammy red fruit, unctuous yet silky, good acidity, strong earthy mineral and a long concentrated jammy black fruit driven finish with earth and cinnamon at the end. It gets sweeter and more complex with air. Pleasant surprise. (95 pts.)

Red Flight 3

  • 1989 Domaine Jean-Louis Chave Hermitage - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Hermitage
    Youthful nose displaying subtle slightly dry red fruit, dry cherry, cherry liqueur, raspberry, rare charred steak, tapenade, leather, garriigue, wild flowers and limestone mineral. There is a slight hint of brett which is a shame. Fully resolved palate, finely layered red fruit, silky and polished, good acidity and mineral and a medium subtle red fruit driven finish with a hint of brett and cinnamon at the end. (93 pts.)
  • 1983 Domaine Jean-Louis Chave Hermitage - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Hermitage
    From a half bottle. Served blind by me and some thinks Burgundy. Burgundian nose displaying subtle red fruit, dry cherry, fresh cherry, rose, pepper, beef jerky, garrigue, tapenade, cinnamon, ash and limestone. Fully resolved palate, subtle red fruit, silky and fluid, bright acidity, strong limestone mineral and a medium to long subtle red fruit driven finish with rose and mineral at the end. Although enjoyable, it needs to be drunk up. (93 pts.)
  • 1990 Château Rayas Châteauneuf-du-Pape Pignan Reserve - France, Rhône, Southern Rhône, Châteauneuf-du-Pape
    Youthful nose displaying decadent black fruit, black cherry preserves, a hint of fruitcake, tobacco, leather, dark spices and earth. Excellent concentration, packed ripe black fruit, medium acidity, strong earthy mineral and a long decadent slightly dry black fruit driven finish with tobacco and cinnamon at the end. This youthful example can improve further for another decade. (94 pts.)

Red Flight 14

  • 1998 Domaine Jamet Côte-Rôtie - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Côte-Rôtie
    Expressive hedonistic nose displaying crushed blackberry, black cherry, blackberry liqueur, lavender, black pepper, tapenade and crushed rock. Beautifully integrated palate, finely layered decadent yet pure black fruit, silky and fluid, perfect amount of acidity, strong mineral and a long pure black fruit driven finish with lavender and black pepper at the end. Drinking incredibly well. (97 pts.)

As usual, a great time was had by all and the lunch ended at 8 pm.
Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Nice notes. It’s amazing how the 1998 Jamet has come into its own in the last few years. Also good to hear positive things about old Pignan, just bought some 1989 for my cellar. Jouan has lovely vine material in CSD and Clos Sorbé. Not surprising that Drouhin buy a lot of the production every year.

posts.cooked:
1983 Domaine Jean-Louis Chave Hermitage[/url] - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Hermitage
… it needs to be drunk up. (93 pts.)

That’s too bad. Guess no Hermitage lasts forever

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
Nice notes. It’s amazing how the 1998 Jamet has come into its own in the last few years. Also good to hear positive things about old Pignan, just bought some 1989 for my cellar. Jouan has lovely vine material in CSD and Clos Sorbé. Not surprising that Drouhin buy a lot of the production every year.

IMO, the 90 vintage in CDP is a bit overrated and I prefer the 89. The Jouan was really nice.

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
1983 Domaine Jean-Louis Chave Hermitage[/url] - France, Rhône, Northern Rhône, Hermitage
… it needs to be drunk up. (93 pts.)

That’s too bad. Guess no Hermitage lasts forever

In general, Rhone wines seem to age faster than Bordeaux and Burgundy. This was a half bottle.

posts.name: Laurent Gibet
posts.cooked:
Kevin,

May 2005, Grange des Pères red verticale (report by Jacques Prandi) :
11. Vin de Pays de l’Hérault 1992 :
DS16,5 – PC16 – MS17 – JP16,5. Note moyenne du groupe : 16,5 - Prix : 143 €
Robe très évoluée, avec des reflets marron.
Nez très viandé, vieux bois, truffé, profond et fin.
La bouche est tenue, fine, fraîche et avec une belle longueur. L’équilibre est superbe avec beaucoup de finesse pour un vin de très bonne tenue.

The best vintage, ex-aequo with a very good 1995.

By the way, I consider Grange des Pères (Laurent Vaillé) and Peyre-Rose (Marlène Soria) as two of the best Languedoc wines.
My ratings however stop at 17/20.
A way to say that the best Languedoc wines cannot rellay challenge the best wines of other prestigious AOC (Châteauneuf, Côte-Rôtie, Hermitage, Bordeaux, Bourgogne).


With similar cellaring, Chave red 1983 should be more solid in bottle and of course even more in magnum.

posts.name: Mike Evans
posts.cooked:
Kevin,

May 2005, Grange des Pères red verticale (report by Jacques Prandi) :
11. Vin de Pays de l’Hérault 1992 :
DS16,5 – PC16 – MS17 – JP16,5. Note moyenne du groupe : 16,5 - Prix : 143 €
Robe très évoluée, avec des reflets marron.
Nez très viandé, vieux bois, truffé, profond et fin.
La bouche est tenue, fine, fraîche et avec une belle longueur. L’équilibre est superbe avec beaucoup de finesse pour un vin de très bonne tenue.

The best vintage, ex-aequo with a very good 1995.

By the way, I consider Grange des Pères (Laurent Vaillé) and Peyres-Roses (Marlène Soria) as two of the best Languedoc wines.
My ratings however stop at 17/20.
A way to say that the best Languedoc wines cannot rellay challenge the best wines of other prestigious AOC (Châteauneuf, Côte-Rôtie, Hermitage, Bordeaux, Bourgogne).


With similar cellaring, Chave red 1983 should be more solid in bottle and of course even more in magnum.

Laurent, have you had the 1993 or 1995 Peyre Roses in recent years? I had all of them 4 years ago and definitely would have put the 1995s among the finest wines I’ve had in the past 5 years:

1995 Domaine de Peyre Rose Coteaux du Languedoc Clos Syrah Leone - France, Languedoc Roussillon, Languedoc, Coteaux du Languedoc (5/24/2014)
A little fleshier than the Clos des Cistes. A steel fist in a velvet glove, instead of a steel fist in a steel glove. Garrigues, tobacco, minerals, pure fruit, it’s a scintillating wine of incredible complexity and depth, and words can’t express how unbelievably good this is. (97 pts.)

1995 Domaine de Peyre Rose Coteaux du Languedoc Clos des Cistes - France, Languedoc Roussillon, Languedoc, Coteaux du Languedoc (5/24/2014)
Stunning garrigues, looks like it could be a new release. Great purity, but tannic and could use 10 more years. Extraordinary. (95 pts.)

1993 Domaine de Peyre Rose Coteaux du Languedoc Clos Syrah Leone - France, Languedoc Roussillon, Languedoc, Coteaux du Languedoc (5/24/2014)
Wow. Just wow. (94 pts.)

1993 Domaine de Peyre Rose Coteaux du Languedoc Clos des Cistes - France, Languedoc Roussillon, Languedoc, Coteaux du Languedoc (5/24/2014)
Drinks great, but young. Pure, concentrated without being overextracted. Incredible, and still needs time. (93 pts.)

1991 Domaine de Peyre Rose Coteaux du Languedoc Clos des Cistes - France, Languedoc Roussillon, Languedoc, Coteaux du Languedoc (5/24/2014)
Pleasant, but getting tired. This bottle wasn’t stored particularly well, though, so well-stored bottles may well be much better. (88 pts.)

I don’t claim any great precision to my scoring, but I tend to be very stingy with higher scores and I would definitely put the 1995 Syrah Leone as qualitatively in the same ballpark as 1982 Mouton or 1990 Chave Hermitage, though it reminded me more of a young 1989 Haut Brion with its herbal minerality.

posts.cooked:

… it needs to be drunk up. (93 pts.)

In general, Rhone wines seem to age faster than Bordeaux and Burgundy. This was a half bottle.

the 83 chave hermitage half bottles have been in that range of “need to be drunk up” since the first time I had it 7-8 years ago. THe 750s are still going strong tho

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked:
Nice notes. It’s amazing how the 1998 Jamet has come into its own in the last few years. Also good to hear positive things about old Pignan, just bought some 1989 for my cellar. Jouan has lovely vine material in CSD and Clos Sorbé. Not surprising that Drouhin buy a lot of the production every year.

98 jamet is soooooooo damn good. I feel like if anyone wants to “get” jamet, pop them a 98 jamet.

2013 Louis Latour Corton-Charlemagne - France, Burgundy, Côte de Beaune, Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru
Is it the vintage or Louis Latour? Lean, slightly medium concentration and slightly muted. The overall expression is fresh and balanced but seems to lack energy. Correctly made and enjoyable but a bit pedestrian. (90 pts.)

That’s pretty much louis latour - correctly made but pedestrian. Must be the pasteurization that nukes away anything but conformity?

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
That’s too bad. Guess no Hermitage lasts forever

In general, Rhone wines seem to age faster than Bordeaux and Burgundy. This was a half bottle.

the 83 chave hermitage half bottles have been in that range of “need to be drunk up” since the first time I had it 7-8 years ago. THe 750s are still going strong tho

The 83 in 750 can be very good but no need to wait unless one wants it to be very Burgundian.

posts.name: Kevin_S_hin
posts.cooked:
That’s pretty much louis latour - correctly made but pedestrian. Must be the pasteurization that nukes away anything but conformity?

Apparently only the reds are pasteurized, thought this shows like it was pasteurized.

Haut-Brion 1989 and Chave red 1990 are in my best wines ever.
Actually, I just recently tasted several times the 2002, both Syrah Léone and Clos des Cistes, regularly scoring 17/20, not more (and less than the wonderful Rayas 2002, for example).
